mechanism_narrative: Sustained low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly supporting higher S&P 500 valuations and generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 6.0% signals a tightening labor market and robust economic activity, which drives consumer confidence and corporate earnings, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE is below 6.0%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) in subsequent quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in real sales and housing starts indicate broad-based demand recovery across both consumer durables and the housing sector, predicting positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both rising MoM
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns (accuracy < 55%) over 60-day horizons in 3 out of 4 consecutive quarters, or the correlation between the two series breaks down.

mechanism_narrative: High GDP growth combined with low inflation (CPIAUCSL below 238) creates a 'Goldilocks' environment where earnings growth is supported without triggering aggressive monetary tightening, leading to positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive returns occur in < 50% of triggered instances, or the mechanism fails to outperform a baseline model when inflation exceeds 238 despite high GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production signals immediate supply-side strength and capacity utilization, which translates into positive short-term S&P 500 returns within the next week.
failure_condition: Rank IC for 5-day forward returns is consistently negative or near zero (abs(IC) < 0.01) over a 1-year rolling window, indicating no short-term predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth exceeding 150,000 persons in a single month indicates strong labor demand and wage growth potential, supporting consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns are negative in > 60% of months where payroll growth exceeds 150k, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.1) in a regression of returns on payroll changes.

mechanism_narrative: When real sales growth outpaces GDP growth, it signals that consumer spending is the primary engine of the economy, reducing reliance on volatile investment or export sectors and predicting positive equity returns over a quarter.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth rate > GDPC1 growth rate
failure_condition: The differential signal (RSAFS - GDPC1) shows no correlation with 60-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.02) over a 3-year rolling window, or returns are negative in > 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1.0 million units per month indicate a sustained recovery in the housing sector, which has significant multiplier effects on related industries and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 60-day returns in > 60% of months where housing starts exceed 1.0 million, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for interest rate changes.
